No invite is created

sampleRows: CRITICAL REGRESSION – DC09
devices, videos about search and tower \salvo\talos\
DC09 CMS is visible in the Marketplace with correct status
CMS cannot be saved without required fields
DC09 Account ID is mandatory when a site forwards alarms to DC09
Site cannot be saved if DC09 Account ID is missing

headers:
Software Delivery – Roles & Process Overview

CSM Team (Customer Success Management)
•Communicate with clients and collect requests
•Create and track tickets and share updates and release outcomes with clients

Technical Writer (Part of CSM Team)
•Document approved features and changes after each release
•Prepare and maintain user-facing documentation and guides

QA Lead (Quality Assurance)
•Review, analyze, and classify requests (Bug / Improvement / New Request)
•Prioritize requests, test delivered work, and prepare Release Notes

Development Team
•Implement approved and ready tasks
•Work on a limited number of tasks at the same time 

Dev Lead
•Oversee execution and task distribution
•Ensure technical readiness of releases

Request Flow (Process Flow)
1.Client communicates with CSM
2.CSM creates a ticket
3.QA reviews and prepares the request
4.Dev Lead + QA Lead + CSM Lead define the priority
5.Development implements
6.QA tests
7.Release + Release Notes

Request Types
•Bug: A malfunction in functionality that was previously working
•Improvement: Enhancement to an existing feature
•New Request: A new feature or capability

Priority Order
1.Critical Bugs
2.Normal Bugs
3.Shared Improvements
4.Client-Specific Requests

1️⃣ Client → Execution Flow
Step 1: Client → CSM
All client communication should go through the CSM only
Direct communication between Dev or QA Lead and the client is not allowed

Step 2: CSM Request Documentation (Mandatory)
Every request must be documented as a Ticket including:
1.Client Name
2.Type: Bug / Improvement / New Request
3.Description: clear and detailed
4.Screenshots / Video (if available)

Impact:
Is this a Blocker? 
Is the client blocked? 
Any related side feature? 
**Any request without these details will be sent back to the CSM

Step 3: QA Lead Review (Gate #1)
QA Lead responsibilities:

1.Understand the request
2.Rewrite it in technical terms
3.Verify whether it is
A real Bug or Misuse 
4.Determine
Risk , Impact on other clients
Outcome:
A clean Ticket ready for Dev execution

Step 4: Prioritization Meeting (30 mins)
Occurs once or twice a week
Attendees: QA Lead, Dev Lead, CSM Lead

Tasks:
Prioritize requests:
* Critical Bugs
* General Bugs
* Shared Improvements
* Client-specific Requests

Goal: Prevent chaos and ensure fair prioritization

2️⃣ Handling Bugs:
* Critical Bug
System Down or client blocked

Fast process:
QA Lead identifies the bug
Ticket marked with Critical label
Moves directly to Kanban Flow
CSM informs the client that the issue is being handled

* Normal Bug
Does not stop work
Placed in Backlog / Kanban Flow according to priority

3️⃣ Client-specific New Requirements
For any client-specific request:
1.QA Lead + Dev perform Quick Impact Analysis
Will it affect other clients?
Is a Feature Flag required?
2.Decision:
 Reject , Postpone ,  Accept as Config / Optional Feature
3. Important:
No client-specific version should exist

4️⃣ Protecting Dev Team
*No direct interruptions
*Everything is handled via Backlog / Kanban Flow
*Only Critical Bugs interrupt the normal flow
